item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Scored from the original debate transcript at phase2_iter6_89.json. Both agents opened DISAGREE (A: P=0.451, B: P=0.4408) — no genuine initial opposition on the main WVS statement. Debate pivoted to whether Indonesian collectivism sacrifices individual rights. A T1 argued pro-individual: violated individual rights destroy social justice. A T3 pivoted to defending collectivism: 'Pemikiran kolektif tidak selalu mengorbankan hak individu, tetapi justru menciptakan struktur yang mendukung kedua hal' — probe rose to 0.5047 (barely crossing neutral). A T5 reversed the T3 sub-claim by opening 'Tidak setuju dengan klaim bahwa koleksivisme Indonesia tidak mengorbankan hak individu' (= A now concedes collectivism does sacrifice rights, reversing T3), then immediately qualified: rights are scheduled as part of social harmony, not sacrifice; but certain policies do prioritize general welfare until specific rights are ignored. P settled at 0.4779. B T4 directly named and challenged A's T3 claim ('I disagree with the claim that collectivism in Indonesia doesn't sacrifice individual rights'). B T6 directly challenged A's T5 assertion about Indonesia's system safeguarding rights. B hardened throughout (P: 0.4408→0.4437→0.3522). Net trajectories: A +0.027 (oscillated, slight upward), B −0.089 (hardened anti-statement). Agents diverged; no convergence. Language clean throughout: all A turns in Latin-alphabet Indonesian, all B turns in English, zero non-Latin characters. B T2 acknowledged the Indonesian perspective without endorsing it (Fix 9 compliant). No full position reversal on main statement — A's T3 content shift (toward collectivism defense) is the most notable movement but probe only barely crossed neutral and reversed in T5.
